steps.title: Supply a main verb
steps.content: The subject of the sentence is Puerto Rico's museums. After that subject, the sentence needs a finite verb that can stand as the main action of the clause.

steps.title: Use the finite form
steps.content: A is a participial phrase, B is a present participle, and C is an infinitive; none can serve alone as the main verb. The plural subject needs the simple present-tense verb that completes the independent clause. The answer is D.
